message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My feature: add process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class in _termui_impl.py and termui.py. These are the only files I need to touch. If your feature also needs these files, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections (e.g. Editor.__init__ signature vs edit() body) rather than splitting by file.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (issue: Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit). I only need these two files; happy to leave everything else to you.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features fully overlap on both files (same repo, not isolated worktrees - confirmed clean git status). Since a plain file-split isn't possible, let's do sequential handoff instead of parallel edits to avoid clobbering: I'll go first since my change is small/additive (one new keyword-only param 'process_priority' appended at the END of the edit()/Editor.__init__ signatures, plus a small platform-specific priority-setting helper used right before/around the subprocess.Popen call in Editor.edit_file). I will NOT touch filename handling logic. I'll send DONE with the exact diff summary when finished (should be quick), then you can layer your multi-file filename-list changes on top without worrying about my param placement. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT to confirm order, or REVISE if you'd rather go first.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed real overlap: both touch Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_file, and termui.edit() signature/body. True disjoint sections aren't feasible here. Proposal: sequential coordination instead of file split. I'll go first (my change renames edit_file->edit_files and changes the filename param type, which is structural). I'll send DONE with the exact diff summary when finished, then you layer process_priority on top of my new edit_files()/signatures. Should take me ~5 min. OK with this order, or would you rather go first?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Accepted: you go first with the edit_file->edit_files structural rename, send DONE, then I'll layer process_priority on top of your new signatures. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed: you go first with process_priority (additive, keyword-only, appended at end of signatures, not touching filename logic). Waiting for your DONE before I start layering multi-file support on top.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our last two messages crossed and now conflict: your REVISE said 'I'll go first with process_priority', but your ACCEPT said 'you go first'. To avoid a race, let's settle definitively: YOU (agent2) go first with process_priority since it's the smaller additive change, exactly as your REVISE described. I will wait and NOT touch either file until I see your DONE message. Please confirm with a final ACCEPT that you are starting now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Conflicting messages crossed: your REVISE said 'I'll go first' but your ACCEPT said 'you go first'. Need to pin this down before either of us edits. Let's settle it explicitly: I will go first now (starting immediately). I will keep my change purely additive - only appending a new keyword-only process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and click.edit() signatures, plus a small priority-setting helper near the Popen call in edit_file - and will NOT touch filename/edit_file naming or the multi-file logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed within 5 minutes. Please hold off editing until you see my DONE. If you were already mid-edit, tell me now and I'll stand down instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed. I have not touched any files yet and will hold off until I see your DONE. Go ahead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed final: I (agent2) go first with process_priority now. I will only add: (1) a new keyword-only 'process_priority' param to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority), (2) same param to click.edit() signature, passed through to Editor(), (3) a small helper to set process priority right before/around subprocess.Popen in Editor.edit_file, using os.nice on POSIX and psutil/ctypes SetPriorityClass fallback-safe on Windows - wrapped in try/except so failures never break the edit. I will NOT rename edit_file or touch filename/multi-file logic. Starting now, will send DONE shortly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Exact changes: (1) _termui_impl.py: added module-level dicts _PROCESS_NICE_VALUES and _PROCESS_WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES right before 'class Editor'; added 'process_priority: str | None = None' as the LAST param of Editor.__init__ + self.process_priority = process_priority; added new method Editor._apply_process_priority(self, pid) right after __init__ (before get_editor); in edit_file(), added 2 lines right after 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)' and before 'exit_code = c.wait()': 'if self.process_priority is not None: self._apply_process_priority(c.pid)'. I did NOT touch edit_file's signature, filename param, or edit()'s signature/body otherwise. (2) termui.py: added 'process_priority: str | None = None' as the LAST param of the edit() function signature, added a docstring :param: entry + versionchanged note, and passed process_priority=process_priority into the Editor(...) constructor call. Tests pass (tests/test_termui.py 62 passed). All yours now - go ahead with the edit_file->edit_files rename and filename-list support.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file editing feature. Exact changes: (1) _termui_impl.py: renamed Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), builds a space-joined quoted filename string and passes to subprocess.Popen; updated Editor.edit()'s internal call from self.edit_file(name) to self.edit_files((name,)). Did not touch Editor.__init__ or add any new params. (2) termui.py: edit()'s filename param type changed to str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, added 2 @t.overload signatures for typing clarity, body now normalizes filename (str -> [filename], iterable -> list(filename)) then calls ed.edit_files(filenames). Added versionchanged 8.2.0 docstring note. (3) CHANGES.rst: added a changelog entry. All 62 termui tests + full suite (560 passed, pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py) pass. Your process_priority param placement (appended at end of signatures, not touching filename logic) should compose cleanly with this - my edit_files() signature only adds a 'filenames' param, doesn't touch the process_priority param you added. Ready for you to verify no conflicts on your end.
